design: Pre-COVID placebo-in-space DiD on Grand Central stations (external validity of the Lumo finding).
hull_note: Hull Trains (2000) launched before the 2004 LENNON panel start -> no clean pre-period, not tested.
interpretation: Grand Central's pre-COVID entries grew their served stations above comparable clean donors (Sunderland +73%, Bradford +49% vs donor medians ~+31%/+20%), with NO pandemic confounding. BUT as single-station placebo tests NEITHER clears conventional significance (placebo p~0.18-0.19; ~1 in 5 random donors grew at least as much). So this is SUGGESTIVE, directionally-consistent external validity — NOT a significant second test. The Lumo OD-flow analysis remains the decisive evidence; GC merely shows the sign generalises pre-COVID.
